---
title: "ERIM Logistic Regression"
format: html
---
## Load cleaned data
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

myData = pd.read_csv("/Users/danielkwong/Downloads/df_prepped.csv")

print(myData.head())
print(myData.shape)
```

We are using **logistic regression** to capture purchase decision for **yogurt** and **frozen dinner** based on information about the household from the ERIM dataset. This means that we will be setting the cutoff for the logistic regression models at **YogExp > 0** and **DinExp > 0**. 

## Create the binary target variables
```{python}
# yogurt purchase indicator
myData["YogBuyer"] = (myData["YogExp"] > 0).astype(int)

# frozen dinner purchase indicator
myData["DinBuyer"] = (myData["DinExp"] > 0).astype(int)

print(myData[["YogExp", "DinExp", "YogBuyer", "DinBuyer"]].sample(10))
```

## Select predictor variables 
```{python}
predictors = [
"HHNbr",
"MWrkHrs",
"FWrkHrs",
"MEdu",
"FEdu",
"FAge",
"MAge",
"F_Rel",
"M_Rel_2",
"ResType_2",
"ResType_3",
"ResType_4",
"ResType_5",
"ResType_6",
"ResStatus_2",
"ResStatus_3",
"Cable_1",
"Cats_1",
"Dogs_1",
"HHInc_2",
"HHInc_3",
"HHInc_4",
"HHInc_5",
"HHInc_6",
"HHInc_7",
"HHInc_8",
"HHInc_9",
"HHInc_10",
"HHInc_11",
"HHInc_12",
"HHInc_13",
"HHInc_14"
]

X = myData[predictors]
```

## Define target variables 
```{python}
yYog = myData["YogBuyer"]
yDin = myData["DinBuyer"]
```

## Train/test split
```{python}
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

Xtrain, Xtest, yYogTrain, yYogTest = train_test_split(
    X, yYog, test_size=0.3, random_state=1
)

_, _, yDinTrain, yDinTest = train_test_split(
    X, yDin, test_size=0.3, random_state=1
)
```

## Fit logistic regression models
```{python}
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

yogModel = LogisticRegression(max_iter=10000)
yogModel.fit(Xtrain, yYogTrain)

dinModel = LogisticRegression(max_iter=10000)
dinModel.fit(Xtrain, yDinTrain)
```

## Predict probabilities 
```{python}
yogProb = yogModel.predict_proba(Xtest)[:,1]
dinProb = dinModel.predict_proba(Xtest)[:,1]
```

## Predict classes
```{python}
yogPred = yogModel.predict(Xtest)
dinPred = dinModel.predict(Xtest)
```

## Model evaluation
```{python}
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, accuracy_score, roc_auc_score

print("Yogurt Model")
print(confusion_matrix(yYogTest, yogPred))
print("Accuracy:", accuracy_score(yYogTest, yogPred))
print("AUC:", roc_auc_score(yYogTest, yogProb))

print("\nFrozen Dinner Model")
print(confusion_matrix(yDinTest, dinPred))
print("Accuracy:", accuracy_score(yDinTest, dinPred))
print("AUC:", roc_auc_score(yDinTest, dinProb))
```

## ROC curve graph
```{python}
from sklearn.metrics import roc_curve, auc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# compute ROC values
fprYog, tprYog, _ = roc_curve(yYogTest, yogProb)
fprDin, tprDin, _ = roc_curve(yDinTest, dinProb)

# compute AUC
aucYog = auc(fprYog, tprYog)
aucDin = auc(fprDin, tprDin)

# plot
plt.figure(figsize=(7,6))

plt.plot(fprYog, tprYog, label=f"Yogurt Model (AUC = {aucYog:.3f})")
plt.plot(fprDin, tprDin, label=f"Frozen Dinner Model (AUC = {aucDin:.3f})")

# random classifier line
plt.plot([0,1], [0,1], linestyle="--")

plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("ROC Curve – Logistic Regression Models")
plt.legend()

plt.show()
```

## Feature importance for Yogurt Purchase
```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# get coefficients
coef = pd.Series(yogModel.coef_[0], index=X.columns)
coef = coef.sort_values()

# color bars based on sign
colors = ["darkred" if c < 0 else "steelblue" for c in coef]

plt.figure(figsize=(8,12))

bars = plt.barh(coef.index, coef.values, color=colors)

# x-axis ticks every 0.1
xmin = coef.min() - 0.1
xmax = coef.max() + 0.1
ticks = np.arange(round(xmin,1), round(xmax,1)+0.1, 0.1)
plt.xticks(ticks)

# vertical dotted gridlines
plt.grid(axis="x", linestyle=":", color="lightgrey")

# bold vertical zero line
plt.axvline(0, color="black", linewidth=1)

# add coefficient labels
for i, v in enumerate(coef.values):
    
    if v >= 0:
        plt.text(v + 0.02, i, f"{v:.2f}", va="center")
    else:
        plt.text(v - 0.02, i, f"{v:.2f}", va="center", ha="right")

plt.xlabel("Coefficient Value")
plt.title("Drivers of Yogurt Purchase")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Feature importance for Frozen Dinner Purchase
```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# get coefficients
coef = pd.Series(dinModel.coef_[0], index=X.columns)
coef = coef.sort_values()

# color bars based on sign
colors = ["darkred" if c < 0 else "steelblue" for c in coef]

plt.figure(figsize=(8,12))

plt.barh(coef.index, coef.values, color=colors)

# x-axis ticks every 0.1
xmin = coef.min() - 0.1
xmax = coef.max() + 0.1
ticks = np.arange(round(xmin,1), round(xmax,1) + 0.1, 0.1)
plt.xticks(ticks)

# light grey dotted vertical lines
plt.grid(axis="x", linestyle=":", color="lightgrey")

# zero line
plt.axvline(0, color="black", linewidth=1)

# add coefficient labels
for i, v in enumerate(coef.values):
    if v >= 0:
        plt.text(v + 0.02, i, f"{v:.2f}", va="center")
    else:
        plt.text(v - 0.02, i, f"{v:.2f}", va="center", ha="right")

plt.xlabel("Coefficient Value")
plt.title("Drivers of Frozen Dinner Purchase")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
